   140ŌåÆnoncomputable def indepNumK (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
   141ŌåÆ  sSup {n | Ōłā f : Fin k ŌåÆ Set V, (ŌłĆ i, G.IsIndepSet (f i)) Ōł¦ (Ōŗā i, f i).ncard = n}
   142ŌåÆ
   143ŌåÆ/-- A finite graph is CDS-colorable if it has a proper coloring
   144ŌåÆby natural numbers such that for all `k > 0`, the number of
   145ŌåÆvertices with color `< k` equals the maximum size of
   146ŌåÆthe union of `k` independent sets. -/
   147ŌåÆdef CDSColorable [Fintype ╬▒] {G : SimpleGraph ╬▒} : Prop :=
   148ŌåÆ    Ōłā (C : G.Coloring Nat), ŌłĆ k : Nat,
   149ŌåÆ   Ōłæ i < k, (C.colorClass i).ncard = indepNumK G k
   150ŌåÆ
   151ŌåÆ/-- A homomorphism is rainbow if it maps distinct edges to distinct colors. -/
   152ŌåÆdef IsRainbow {╬▒ V : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph ╬▒} {G : SimpleGraph V} (f : H ŌåÆg G) {C : Type*}
   153ŌåÆ    (c : Sym2 V ŌåÆ C) : Prop :=
   154ŌåÆ  Function.Injective fun e : H.edgeSet => c (Sym2.map f e)
   155ŌåÆ
   156ŌåÆ/--
   157ŌåÆThe anti-Ramsey number $\mathrm{AR}(n, H)$: maximum colors to edge-color $K_n$ without rainbow $H$.
   158ŌåÆ-/
   159ŌåÆnoncomputable def antiRamseyNum {╬▒ : Type*} [Fintype ╬▒] (H : SimpleGraph ╬▒) (n : ŌäĢ) : ŌäĢ :=
   160ŌåÆ  sSup {k | Ōłā c : Sym2 (Fin n) ŌåÆ Fin k, ŌłĆ f : H ŌåÆg ŌŖż, ┬¼IsRainbow f c}

    24ŌåÆ
    25ŌåÆThis file defines the size Ramsey number for simple graphs.
    26ŌåÆ
    27ŌåÆ## Definition
    28ŌåÆ
    29ŌåÆThe size Ramsey number `r╠é(G, H)` is the minimum number of edges in a graph `F`
    30ŌåÆsuch that any 2-coloring of `F`'s edges contains a copy of `G` in one color or `H` in the other.
    31ŌåÆ-/
    32ŌåÆ
    33ŌåÆnamespace SimpleGraph
    34ŌåÆ
    35ŌåÆ/--
    36ŌåÆThe size Ramsey number `r╠é(G, H)` is the minimum number of edges in a graph `F`
    37ŌåÆsuch that any 2-coloring of `F`'s edges contains a copy of `G` in one color or `H` in the other.
    38ŌåÆ
    39ŌåÆA 2-coloring is represented by a subgraph `R Ōēż F` (the "red" edges); the "blue" edges are `F \ R`.
    40ŌåÆ-/
    41ŌåÆnoncomputable def sizeRamsey {╬▒ ╬▓ : Type*} [Fintype ╬▒] [Fintype ╬▓]
    42ŌåÆ    (G : SimpleGraph ╬▒) (H : SimpleGraph ╬▓) : ŌäĢ :=
    43ŌåÆ  sInf { m | Ōłā (n : ŌäĢ) (F : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    44ŌåÆ    F.edgeSet.ncard = m Ōł¦
    45ŌåÆ    ŌłĆ (R : SimpleGraph (Fin n)), R Ōēż F ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆ      G.IsContained R Ōł© H.IsContained (F \ R) }
    47ŌåÆ
    48ŌåÆ/--

    50ŌåÆfor all graphs `H` with `m` edges and no isolated vertices, `r╠é(G, H) Ōēż c ┬Ę m`.
    51ŌåÆ-/

    53ŌåÆ  Ōłā c > (0 : ŌäØ), ŌłĆ (n : ŌäĢ) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel H.Adj],
    54ŌåÆ    (ŌłĆ v, 0 < H.degree v) ŌåÆ
    55ŌåÆ    (sizeRamsey G H : ŌäØ) Ōēż c * H.edgeSet.ncard

    30ŌåÆ* There is a blue clique of cardinality `c`.

    32ŌåÆdef OrdinalCardinalRamsey (╬▒ ╬▓ : Ordinal.{u}) (c : Cardinal.{u}) : Prop :=
    33ŌåÆ  -- For any 2-coloring of `╬▒`,

    36ŌåÆ    (Ōłā s, red.IsClique s Ōł¦ typeLT s = ╬▓) Ōł©
    37ŌåÆ   -- or there is a blue `K_c`.
    38ŌåÆ    Ōłā s, blue.IsClique s Ōł¦ #s = c

    19ŌåÆ/-!
    20ŌåÆ# Erd┼æs Problem 8
    21ŌåÆ
    22ŌåÆ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/8](https://www.erdosproblems.com/8)
    23ŌåÆ
    24ŌåÆThe original Erd┼æsŌĆōGraham conjecture asked: for any finite colouring of the positive integers,
    25ŌåÆmust there exist a covering system all of whose moduli are monochromatic?
    26ŌåÆ
    27ŌåÆThe answer is **no**, as a consequence of Hough's theorem [Ho15] that every covering system
    28ŌåÆmust contain a modulus below an absolute bound (at most $10^{16}$, later improved to $616000$
    29ŌåÆby Balister, Bollob├Īs, Morris, Sahasrabudhe, and Tiba [BBMST22]). One can therefore assign a
    30ŌåÆdistinct colour to each integer up to this bound and a single fresh colour to all larger integers;
    31ŌåÆany covering system must then contain a small modulus with a unique colour, making
    32ŌåÆmonochromaticity impossible.
    33ŌåÆ
    34ŌåÆ[Ho15] Hough, R. D., _The interval of covering congruences_. Ann. of Math. (2) **181** (2015),
    35ŌåÆno. 1, 361ŌĆō382.
    36ŌåÆ
    37ŌåÆ[BBMST22] Balister, P., Bollob├Īs, B., Morris, R., Sahasrabudhe, J., and Tiba, M.,
    38ŌåÆ_The Erd┼æs covering problem: the density of the uncovered set_. Ann. of Math. (2) **198** (2023),
    39ŌåÆno. 1, 1ŌĆō92.
    40ŌåÆ-/
    41ŌåÆ
    42ŌåÆnamespace Erdos8
    43ŌåÆ
    44ŌåÆ/--
    45ŌåÆA finite system of congruences $\{(a_i, m_i)\}$ is a **covering system** if every
    46ŌåÆmodulus is positive and every integer satisfies at least one congruence $n \equiv a_i \pmod{m_i}$.
    47ŌåÆ-/
    48ŌåÆdef IsCoveringSystem (S : Finset (Ōäż ├Ś ŌäĢ)) : Prop :=
    49ŌåÆ  S.Nonempty Ōł¦
    50ŌåÆ  (ŌłĆ p Ōłł S, 0 < p.2) Ōł¦
    51ŌåÆ  (ŌłĆ n : Ōäż, Ōłā p Ōłł S, (p.2 : Ōäż) ŌłŻ (n - p.1))
    52ŌåÆ
    53ŌåÆ/--
    54ŌåÆAll moduli in a covering system are **monochromatic** under a colouring $\chi : \mathbb{N} \to \text{Fin}\, k$
    55ŌåÆif there exists a colour $c$ such that every modulus in $S$ receives colour $c$.
    56ŌåÆ-/
    57ŌåÆdef HasMonochromaticModuli {k : ŌäĢ} (Žć : ŌäĢ ŌåÆ Fin k) (S : Finset (Ōäż ├Ś ŌäĢ)) : Prop :=
    58ŌåÆ  Ōłā c : Fin k, ŌłĆ p Ōłł S, Žć p.2 = c
    59ŌåÆ
    60ŌåÆ/--
    61ŌåÆThe Erd┼æsŌĆōGraham conjecture asked: for any finite colouring of the positive integers,
    62ŌåÆmust there exist a covering system all of whose moduli are monochromatic?

    65ŌåÆmust contain a modulus below an absolute bound.
    66ŌåÆ-/

    70ŌåÆ      Ōłā S : Finset (Ōäż ├Ś ŌäĢ), IsCoveringSystem S Ōł¦ HasMonochromaticModuli Žć S) := by
    71ŌåÆ  sorry
    72ŌåÆ

    24ŌåÆ/-! # Arithmetic Progressions
    25ŌåÆ
    26ŌåÆMain definitions:
    27ŌåÆ- `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set ╬▒) (l : ŌäĢŌł×) (a d : ╬▒)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the
    28ŌåÆ  set consisting of an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite) with first term
    29ŌåÆ  `a` and difference `d`. Useful for cases in which additional conditions need to be applied to
    30ŌåÆ  the individual terms and/or difference.
    31ŌåÆ- `Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set ╬▒) (l : ŌäĢŌł×)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the set consisting
    32ŌåÆ  of an arithmetic progression of length `l`, for some some first term and difference.
    33ŌåÆ-/
    34ŌåÆ
    35ŌåÆvariable {╬▒ : Type*} [AddCommMonoid ╬▒]
    36ŌåÆ
    37ŌåÆ/--
    38ŌåÆA set $S$ is an arithmetic progression of length $l$ with first term $a$ and difference $d$
    39ŌåÆif $S = \{a, a + d, ..., a + (l - 1)d\}$, if $l$ if finite, else $S = \{a, a + d, a + 2d, ...\}.
    40ŌåÆThis can be written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith a l a d`, where `l : ŌäĢŌł×` may take the infinite
    41ŌåÆvalue `ŌŖż`.
    42ŌåÆ-/
    43ŌåÆdef Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set ╬▒) (l : ŌäĢŌł×) (a d : ╬▒) : Prop :=
    44ŌåÆ  ENat.card s = l Ōł¦ s = {a + n ŌĆó d | (n : ŌäĢ) (_ : n < l)}
    45ŌåÆ
    46ŌåÆ/--
    47ŌåÆA list version of `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
    48ŌåÆwhen considering images under arbitrary functions.
    49ŌåÆ-/
    50ŌåÆdef List.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : List ╬▒) (l : ŌäĢ) (a d : ╬▒) : Prop :=
    51ŌåÆ  s = (List.range l).map (fun n Ōå” a + n ŌĆó d) Ōł© s = (List.range l).reverse.map (fun n Ōå” a + n ŌĆó d)
    52ŌåÆ
    53ŌåÆnamespace Set.IsAPOfLengthWith

    55ŌåÆvariable {s : Set ╬▒} {l : ŌäĢŌł×} {a d : ╬▒}
    56ŌåÆ
    57ŌåÆtheorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : ENat.card s = l := h.1
    58ŌåÆtheorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s = {a + n ŌĆó d | (n : ŌäĢ) (_ : n < l)} := h.2
    59ŌåÆ
    60ŌåÆ/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    61ŌåÆif `s` is empty. -/
    62ŌåÆ@[simp]
    63ŌåÆtheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d Ōåö s = Ōłģ := by simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    64ŌåÆ
    65ŌåÆ/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    66ŌåÆif `s` is a singleton. -/
    67ŌåÆ@[simp]
    68ŌåÆtheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d Ōåö s = {a} := by simp +contextual [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    69ŌåÆ
    70ŌåÆend Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    71ŌåÆ
    72ŌåÆnamespace List.IsAPOfLengthWith
    73ŌåÆ

    78ŌåÆ
    79ŌåÆ/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    80ŌåÆif `s` is empty. -/
    81ŌåÆ@[simp]
    82ŌåÆtheorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d Ōåö s = [] := by
    83ŌåÆ  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    84ŌåÆ
    85ŌåÆ/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only

    87ŌåÆ@[simp]
    88ŌåÆtheorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d Ōåö s = [a] := by

    91ŌåÆend List.IsAPOfLengthWith

    94ŌåÆlength `2` with first term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/
